buggy_code:
features = features[~np.isnan(outcome)]
outcome = outcome[~np.isnan(outcome)]
outcome = outcome[np.all(~np.isnan(features), axis=1)]
features = features[np.all(~np.isnan(features), axis=1)]
clean_code:
# Filter both arrays using a single shared mask computed once
valid_outcome = ~np.isnan(outcome)
valid_features = np.all(~np.isnan(features), axis=1)
combined_mask = valid_outcome & valid_features
features = features[combined_mask]
outcome = outcome[combined_mask]
description: Features and outcome are filtered with separate masks applied sequentially, causing index misalignment.
explanation: Line 1 filters features using outcome's NaN mask, changing features' shape. Line 2 filters outcome using the ORIGINAL outcome mask — which is now applied to the already-filtered features array. After this, features and outcome have been filtered by different effective masks and are silently misaligned. The model trains on wrong (feature, label) pairs.
suggested_fix: Compute a single combined boolean mask from both arrays before filtering. Apply it once to both features and outcome.

issues.title: Silent misalignment from sequential independent NaN filtering
